title: Failure analysis, robustness, and stress testing
rq: Which design choices in Failure analysis, robustness, and stress testing drive the major trade-offs, and how are those trade-offs measured?
thesis: For Failure analysis, robustness, and stress testing, failure taxonomy and stress scenarios and robustness criteria under deployment shift is a recurring axis of variation, and results are easiest to interpret when protocols and failure assumptions are explicit.

include: Core topics directly relevant to 'Failure analysis, robustness, and stress testing'.
scope_rule.notes: If you include an out-of-scope paper as a bridge, state the reason in 1 sentence and keep it secondary.

tension_statement: In Failure analysis, robustness, and stress testing, the central tension is broad stress coverage versus actionable diagnosis: wider robustness testing reveals more failure modes, but it can also blur which mechanism or deployment factor actually caused the breakdown.
